description_long: This object consists of two adjacent multi-story wooden houses built on a sloped terrain, each featuring a prominent hip roof with a thatched appearance. The houses are elevated on stilts at the front, accommodating the uneven ground, and have large wraparound balconies with wooden railings on each level. The exterior walls are clad in wood, and the houses are connected by a shared open stairwell structure. Multiple sets of windows are arranged in vertical rows, providing ample natural light to the interiors. The overall design suggests a tropical or resort-style residential building, with open, airy spaces and outdoor living areas. The green base simulates landscaped ground, contrasting with the dark, cutaway slope.
description: Two multi-story wooden houses with thatched hip roofs, wraparound balconies, and stilts, set on a sloped green terrain. The design features open stairwells, vertical window rows, and a tropical resort-style aesthetic.
description_view_0: Front angled view showing both houses, wraparound balconies, and the sloped terrain with visible stilts.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the houses, the hip roofs, and the staggered placement on the slope.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back facades, vertical window rows, and the connection between the two houses.
description_view_3: Direct front view emphasizing the open balconies, supporting stilts, and the main entry stairwells.
attribute_entryway_design: Open stairwells leading to covered porches with wooden railings and steps, no ornate decoration.
